tasks.title: Setup Blazor Project Structure
tasks.description: Set up the Blazor project structure with server-side static rendering and a WASM client project. Configure Havit.Blazor UI and Bootstrap 5.3.
tasks.details:
1. Create a new Blazor Web App project.
2. Add Havit.Blazor UI NuGet packages.
3. Configure Bootstrap 5.3.
4. Structure the project with feature-based folders (e.g., Creators, Questionnaires).
5. Set up static rendering for the server-side project and configure the WASM client project.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify the project builds successfully and that Havit.Blazor UI components and Bootstrap styling are correctly applied. Check that static rendering is enabled on the server.

tasks.title: Implement Creator Profiles Feature
tasks.description: Implement the Creator Profiles feature with public and studio views. Include support for managing support targets (Twitch, Patreon, YouTube, etc.) and staff management.
tasks.details:
1. Create Creator model and database schema.
2. Implement API endpoints for creator profile management using Immediate.Apis.
3. Develop UI components for public and studio views using Havit.Blazor UI.
4. Implement support target management (add, edit, delete).
5. Implement staff management (invite, manage permissions).
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that creator profiles can be created, updated, and viewed correctly in both public and studio views. Ensure support targets and staff can be managed. Test API endpoints for proper functionality and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Questionnaires Feature
tasks.description: Implement the Questionnaires feature, including creation, editing, versioning, submission, approval workflow, labels, comments, bulk/file operations, and selection series.
tasks.details:
1. Create Questionnaire and Submission models and database schema.
2. Implement API endpoints for questionnaire management using Immediate.Apis.
3. Develop UI components for questionnaire creation, editing, and submission using Havit.Blazor UI.
4. Implement approval workflow (auto-approve, manual approval, closed).
5. Implement labels and threaded comments (public and staff).
6. Implement bulk and file operations (download submissions).
7. Implement selection series management.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that questionnaires can be created, edited, submitted, and approved correctly. Ensure labels, comments, and file operations function as expected. Test API endpoints for proper functionality and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Eligibility Feature
tasks.description: Implement the Eligibility feature, including configuration of eligibility rules and presentation of eligibility status and requirements.
tasks.details:
1. Create EligibilityRule model and database schema.
2. Implement API endpoints for eligibility configuration using Immediate.Apis.
3. Develop UI components for displaying eligibility status and requirements using Havit.Blazor UI.
4. Implement logic for checking eligibility based on defined rules.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that eligibility rules can be configured and that eligibility status is displayed correctly in both public and studio views. Test API endpoints for proper functionality and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Polls Feature
tasks.description: Implement the Polls feature, including poll creation, management, and voting.
tasks.details:
1. Create Poll model and database schema.
2. Implement API endpoints for poll management using Immediate.Apis.
3. Develop UI components for poll creation, editing, and voting using Havit.Blazor UI.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that polls can be created, edited, and voted on correctly. Test API endpoints for proper functionality and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Identity & Authentication
tasks.description: Implement Identity & Authentication using external logins (Discord, Google) and profile management. Implement role-based authorization for creators, staff, and supporters.
tasks.details:
1. Configure external login providers (Discord, Google).
2. Implement user profile management (update profile, link accounts).
3. Implement role-based authorization (creators, staff, supporters).
4. Secure endpoints and UI components based on roles.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that users can log in using external providers and manage their profiles. Ensure role-based authorization is correctly implemented and enforced. Test API endpoints for proper authentication and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement File Management
tasks.description: Implement secure file upload and download endpoints with file type and size restrictions.
tasks.details:
1. Implement API endpoints for file upload and download using Immediate.Apis.
2. Configure file type and size restrictions per questionnaire.
3. Secure file access based on user roles and permissions.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that files can be uploaded and downloaded securely. Ensure file type and size restrictions are enforced. Test API endpoints for proper authentication and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Notifications
tasks.description: Implement notification dispatchers for email and Discord. Notify users and staff on submission/comment/approval events.
tasks.details:
1. Configure email (SMTP) and Discord notification dispatchers.
2. Implement logic for sending notifications on submission, comment, and approval events.
3. Integrate with Discord bot for Discord notifications.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that email and Discord notifications are sent correctly on submission, comment, and approval events. Test Discord bot integration.

tasks.title: Implement Studio (Admin) Tools
tasks.description: Implement Studio (Admin) tools, including a dashboard, breadcrumbs, and navigation using Havit.Blazor components.
tasks.details:
1. Develop studio layouts and navigation using Havit.Blazor UI.
2. Implement a dashboard for managing creators, questionnaires, supporters, and submissions.
3. Implement breadcrumbs for consistent navigation.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that the studio dashboard and navigation are functional and user-friendly. Ensure breadcrumbs are displayed correctly. Test UI components for proper rendering and behavior.

tasks.title: Implement Supporters Feature
tasks.description: Implement the Supporters feature, including viewing and managing supporters, their support status, and participation.
tasks.details:
1. Create Supporter model and database schema.
2. Implement API endpoints for supporter management using Immediate.Apis.
3. Develop UI components for viewing and managing supporters using Havit.Blazor UI.
4. Implement logic for tracking supporter status and participation.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that supporters can be viewed and managed correctly. Ensure supporter status and participation are tracked accurately. Test API endpoints for proper functionality and authorization.

tasks.title: Implement Maximum Submissions Per User Feature
tasks.description: Implement the ability to configure and enforce a maximum number of submissions per user per questionnaire. This includes updating the eligibility option entity, endpoints, UI, and validation logic.
tasks.details:
1. Update the EligibilityOption model to include a `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` property.
2. Modify the questionnaire creation/editing UI in the Studio (Admin) tools to allow creators to set the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value.
3. Update the API endpoints for creating and updating questionnaires to persist the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value.
4. Implement validation logic in the submission process to check if the user has already reached the maximum number of submissions for the questionnaire.
5. If the user has reached the maximum, display an appropriate error message.
6. Document the new feature in the relevant documentation.
7. Ensure that the feature is compatible with existing eligibility rules and submission workflows.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Create questionnaires with different `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` values (e.g., 1, 5, unlimited).
2. As a user, submit questionnaires and verify that the system enforces the maximum submission limit.
3. Ensure that the correct error message is displayed when the user exceeds the limit.
4. Test the feature with different user roles (e.g., creators, staff, supporters).
5. Verify that the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value is correctly displayed in the Studio (Admin) tools.
6. Test the feature with existing eligibility rules to ensure compatibility.
7. Test edge cases, such as submitting questionnaires concurrently or attempting to bypass the validation logic.

subtasks.title: Update EligibilityOption Model
subtasks.description: Modify the `EligibilityOption` data model to include a `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` property. This property will store the maximum number of submissions allowed per user for a given questionnaire.
subtasks.details: Add a new integer property named `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` to the `EligibilityOption` entity. Ensure the property is nullable to indicate no limit. Update database schema accordingly using migrations.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that the new `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` property is correctly added to the `EligibilityOption` model and that database migrations are successful.

subtasks.title: Implement UI for Configuring Max Submissions
subtasks.description: Modify the questionnaire creation/editing UI in the Studio (Admin) tools to allow questionnaire creators to set the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value.
subtasks.details: Add a new input field (e.g., a number input) to the questionnaire creation/editing form in the Studio. This field should allow administrators to specify the maximum number of submissions per user. If the field is left blank or set to 0, it should be interpreted as no limit. Ensure appropriate validation on the input field (e.g., non-negative integer).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test that the new input field is displayed correctly in the UI and that the entered value is properly bound to the questionnaire object. Verify that validation prevents invalid input (e.g., negative numbers).

subtasks.title: Update API Endpoints
subtasks.description: Update the API endpoints for creating and updating questionnaires to persist the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value. This ensures that the value set in the UI is correctly stored in the database.
subtasks.details: Modify the API endpoints responsible for creating and updating questionnaires to accept and persist the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value. Map the value from the request body to the corresponding property in the `EligibilityOption` entity. Handle null or zero values appropriately to indicate no limit.
subtasks.testStrategy: Use API testing tools (e.g., Postman) to send requests to the create and update questionnaire endpoints with different values for `MaxSubmissionsPerUser`. Verify that the values are correctly stored in the database.

subtasks.title: Implement Submission Validation Logic
subtasks.description: Implement validation logic in the submission process to check if the user has already reached the maximum number of submissions for the questionnaire.
subtasks.details: In the submission processing logic, retrieve the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value for the questionnaire. Query the database to count the number of submissions made by the current user for the questionnaire. Compare the submission count with the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value. If the user has reached the maximum, prevent the submission and proceed to the next subtask.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test users and questionnaires with different `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` values. Submit submissions as the test users and verify that the validation logic correctly prevents submissions when the maximum is reached.

subtasks.title: Display Error Message
subtasks.description: If the user has reached the maximum number of submissions, display an appropriate error message to inform them that they cannot submit any more responses.
subtasks.details: When the submission validation logic determines that the user has reached the maximum number of submissions, display a user-friendly error message. The message should clearly explain why the submission was rejected and inform the user about the maximum submission limit.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test users and questionnaires with a `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` value of 1. Submit one submission as the test user and then attempt to submit another. Verify that the correct error message is displayed.

subtasks.title: Document the Feature
subtasks.description: Document the new feature in the relevant documentation. This includes explaining how to configure the maximum submissions per user and how it affects the submission process.
subtasks.details: Update the documentation to include a section describing the `MaxSubmissionsPerUser` feature. Explain how to configure the feature in the Studio UI, how it is persisted in the API, and how it affects the submission process. Include examples and screenshots where appropriate.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review the documentation to ensure that it is clear, accurate, and complete. Verify that all aspects of the feature are adequately documented.
